sampleQuestions.stem: Consider the following problem. Given n positive integers a_1, a_2, ..., a_n, it is required to partition them into two parts A and B such that sum_{a_i in A} a_i - sum_{a_j in B} a_j is minimised. Consider a greedy algo

Match the pairs in the following questions: Column I: (a) Strassen's matrix multiplication algorithm (b) Kruskal's minimum spanning tree algorithm (c) Biconnected components algorithm (d) Floyd's shortest path algorithm
Algorithm design technique used in quicksort algorithm is? A. Dynamic programming B. Backtracking C. Divide and conquer D. Greedy method

An array A of length n with distinct elements is said to be bitonic if there is an index i (1 <= i <= n) such that A[1..i] is sorted in non-decreasing order and A[i..n] is sorted in non-increasing order. Which ONE of the

If one uses straight two-way merge sort algorithm to sort the following elements in ascending order: 6, 10, 13, 5, 8, 3, 2, 7, 9, 1 then the order of these elements after second pass of the algorithm is: A. 6, 13, 5, 10,

A list of n strings, each of length n, is sorted into lexicographic order using the merge-sort algorithm. The worst case running time of this computation is: A. O(n log n) B. O(n^2 log n) C. O(n^2 + log n) D. O(n^2)

Assume that a mergesort algorithm in the worst case takes 30 seconds for an input of size 64. Which of the following most closely approximates the maximum input size of a problem that can be solved in 6 minutes? A. 256 B
For merging two sorted lists of sizes m and n into a sorted list of size m + n, we require comparisons of: A. O(m) B. O(n) C. O(m + n) D. O(log m + log n)

Suppose P, Q, R, S, T are sorted sequences having lengths 20, 24, 30, 35, 50 respectively. They are to be merged into a single sequence by merging together two sequences at a time. The number of comparisons that will be

Let P be a quicksort program to sort numbers in ascending order. Let t_1 and t_2 be the time taken by the program for the inputs [1, 2, 3, 4] and [5, 4, 3, 2, 1], respectively. Which of the following holds? A. t_1 < t_2

An input file has 10 records with keys as given below: 25 7 34 2 70 9 61 16 49 19 This is to be sorted in non-decreasing order. i. Sort the input file using QUICKSORT by correctly positioning the first element of the fil

Assume that the last element of the set is used as partition element in Quicksort. If n distinct elements from the set {1, 2, ..., n} are to be sorted, give an input for which Quicksort takes maximum time.
